challenges.title: Widget Configuration Builder
challenges.description: Create a function called 'build_widget_config' that takes keyword arguments for text, bg (background color), fg (foreground color), and font, and returns a dictionary containing only the keys that were provided (not None). Defaults: text=None, bg=None, fg=None, font=None. Also create a variable 'sample_config' by calling build_widget_config(text='Hello', bg='white', fg='black', font='Arial 12').
challenges.starter_code:
def build_widget_config(text=None, bg=None, fg=None, font=None):
    """Build a config dict from non-None keyword arguments."""
    pass

# Create sample_config with all four options set
sample_config = None
hints: Build a dict of all params, then filter out None values: {k: v for k, v in pairs if v is not None}.
You can use locals() or just explicitly list the params: items = {'text': text, 'bg': bg, ...}

challenges.title: Widget State Machine
challenges.description: Write a class called 'WidgetStateManager' that tracks enable/disable states for named widgets. It should support: add_widget(name) — adds widget in 'enabled' state; enable(name) — sets widget to 'enabled'; disable(name) — sets widget to 'disabled'; is_enabled(name) — returns True/False; get_all_states() — returns dict of {name: state}; toggle(name) — switches between enabled/disabled. All methods that reference a widget name should raise KeyError if the widget hasn't been added.
challenges.test_code:
mgr = WidgetStateManager()
mgr.add_widget('button1')
mgr.add_widget('entry1')
assert mgr.is_enabled('button1') == True
assert mgr.is_enabled('entry1') == True

mgr.disable('button1')
assert mgr.is_enabled('button1') == False
assert mgr.get_all_states() == {'button1': 'disabled', 'entry1': 'enabled'}

mgr.toggle('button1')
assert mgr.is_enabled('button1') == True
mgr.toggle('entry1')
assert mgr.is_enabled('entry1') == False

mgr.enable('entry1')
assert mgr.is_enabled('entry1') == True

# KeyError for unknown widget
try:
    mgr.is_enabled('unknown')
    assert False, 'Should raise KeyError'
except KeyError:
    pass

try:
    mgr.toggle('unknown')
    assert False, 'Should raise KeyError'
except KeyError:
    pass
hints: Use a dict self._states = {} to map widget names to 'enabled'/'disabled' strings.
In each method, check if name is in self._states first. If not, raise KeyError(name).
